title: COSO Principle 5 — Accountability enforcement: segregation-of-duties and access-review evidence
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=COSO, clause=Principle5, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Principle5.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that COSO Principle 5 (Enforces accountability) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.32: COSO Principle 5 — Accountability enforcement: segregation-of-duties and access-review evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.32' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: IAM role-assignment logs, SoD conflict matrix, access-review campaign results.
spl:
index=iam sourcetype IN (role_assignments, sod_violations, access_reviews) earliest=-30d
| eval is_sod_conflict=if(sourcetype="sod_violations", 1, 0)
| eval review_completed=if(sourcetype="access_reviews" AND review_status="completed", 1, 0)
| stats sum(is_sod_conflict) AS sod_violations dc(eval(if(sourcetype="access_reviews", campaign_id, null()))) AS review_campaigns sum(review_completed) AS reviews_completed dc(user) AS users_reviewed BY business_unit
| eval sod_risk=if(sod_violations>0, "elevated", "normal")
| where sod_violations>0 OR reviews_completed=0
| table business_unit, sod_violations, sod_risk, review_campaigns, reviews_completed, users_reviewed
description: Monitors segregation-of-duties violations and access-review completion rates to provide evidence that the organisation enforces accountability per COSO Principle 5.
value: COSO Principle 5 requires that the organisation holds individuals accountable for internal control responsibilities. SoD monitoring and access-review tracking provides auditable evidence of accountability enforcement.
implementation: (1) Ingest IAM role-assignment and SoD-violation events; (2) ingest access-review campaign results; (3) schedule weekly; (4) escalate SoD conflicts to compliance team. Indexes required: `index=iam`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=access_reviews`, `sourcetype=role_assignments`, `sourcetype=sod_violations`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `business_unit`, `is_sod_conflict`, `review_campaigns`, `review_completed`, `reviews_completed`, `sod_risk`.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IAM role-assignment logs, SoD conflict matrix, access-review campaign results.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest IAM role-assignment and SoD-violation events; (2) ingest access-review campaign results; (3) schedule weekly; (4) escalate SoD conflicts to compliance team.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iam sourcetype IN (role_assignments, sod_violations, access_reviews) earliest=-30d
| eval is_sod_conflict=if(sourcetype="sod_violations", 1, 0)
| eval review_completed=if(sourcetype="access_reviews" AND review_status="completed", 1, 0)
| stats sum(is_sod_conflict) AS sod_violations dc(eval(if(sourcetype="access_reviews", campaign_id, null()))) AS review_campaigns sum(review_completed) AS reviews_completed dc(user) AS users_reviewed BY business_unit
| eval sod_risk=if(sod_violations>0, "elevated", "normal")
| where sod_violations>0 OR reviews_completed=0
| table business_unit, sod_violations, sod_risk, review_campaigns, reviews_completed, users_reviewed
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for monitors segregation-of-duties violations and access-review completion rates to provide evidence that the organisation enforces accountability per coso principle 5. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
